source.multipart_basis: SAUVC open-frame AUVs are naturally multi-part: forward and aft circular ring frames, longitudinal tubes tying them, thruster cradles at each face, the pressure-hull mount saddle, a camera / acoustic-array dome mount, and a ballast tray. The single-body brief collapses this into one idealised structure; the multipart file restores the real assembly. Speculative at the level of exact longitudinal count; all load magnitudes, material, and pass/fail thresholds are preserved.

full_prompt:
Design the open-frame chassis for a SAUVC (Singapore AUV Challenge) AUV compliant with SAUVC Rulebook v6. Vehicle bounding box 1500 mm x 800 mm x 500 mm. Pool-depth rated 5 m (SAUVC pool). Vehicle dry mass 35 kg. The frame is a bolted assembly of extruded aluminum 6063-T6 bars (25 mm x 25 mm square cross-section, 3 mm wall) forming a 3D open cage; the frame itself is the subject of design but shall be idealized and submitted as a single STEP assembly treated as one part. Material: 6063-T6 aluminum, E = 68.3 GPa, yield = 214 MPa, ultimate = 241 MPa, density = 2700 kg/m^3. The frame carries: 6 thrusters (mounted at 6 faces of the frame; each applies nominal 50 N static thrust, peak 80 N); 1 pressure hull (8 kg dry); 1 battery pod (4 kg dry); acoustic array (2 kg). Total applied-in-frame mass 35 kg. Load cases: (R1) Handling: the full 35 kg vehicle lifted by a single top handle at mid-length => 35 x 9.81 = 343 N concentrated vertical load with 2x dynamic amplification = 687 N; max von Mises in any frame member <= yield / 2.0 = 107 MPa. (R2) Thruster combined: 6 thrusters at peak 80 N each applied in the worst-combined direction (e.g., 2 surge + 2 sway + 2 heave); max von Mises <= 107 MPa. (R3) Impact: 500 N concentrated load applied to any corner (pool wall strike); max von Mises <= yield / 1.5 = 143 MPa. (R4) Hydrostatic buoyancy: frame shall maintain its geometry within +-0.5 mm over its long axis at 5 m depth (50 kPa external); this is effectively a stiffness check - deflection under hydrostatic differential acting on any enclosed bladder-like members <= 0.5 mm (no closed-volume members in this design, so geometric by inspection). (R5) Frame structural mass <= 4 kg. (R6) Kill-switch accessibility: kill switch mount shall be reachable from any accessible face within 500 mm (geometric). Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static x3). Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 frame assembly as single structural idealization, bolt schedule, and a report listing frame mass, max von Mises per case in MPa.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
SAUVC AUV frames are bolted extrusion cages with discrete sub-assemblies; delivered as a multi-part assembly. Speculated families: (A) forward ring frame (rectangular hoop 800 x 500 from 25 x 25 extrusion at the bow); (B) aft ring frame (rectangular hoop at the stern); (C) longitudinal tubes (x6) tying A and B with corner longitudinals (top, bottom, and midline port/starboard); (D) thruster cradles (x6, one per face: forward, aft, top, bottom, port, starboard), each an aluminum plate with a through-bore and 4 bolts into the adjacent longitudinals or ring; (E) pressure-hull saddle (2-strap + backplate carrying the 8 kg pressure hull through the central longitudinals); (F) camera / acoustic-array dome mount (forward-facing plate bolted to A ring); (G) ballast tray bolted to the bottom longitudinal, carrying battery pod 4 kg + ballast; (H) top lift handle bolted across the top longitudinals (primary LC1 point). Interfaces: A<->C bolted corner brackets (4 per ring); B<->C bolted corner brackets (4 per ring); D<->C/A/B bolted (thruster cradles bolt onto the existing frame); E<->C bolted through the two bottom longitudinals (saddle straps clamp the pressure hull); F<->A bolted plate on the forward ring; G<->C bolted on the bottom longitudinal; H<->C bolted on the top longitudinals. FEM verification runs on the assembled model ONLY per fem_check_policy=assembled_only. Under LC1 the top handle H loads flow through IF7 into the top longitudinals C; under LC2 each thruster reaction from D feeds its cradle into the adjacent longitudinals; LC3 impact at a corner stresses the corner bracket between A/B and C. Load magnitudes (687 N LC1, 80 N x 6 LC2, 500 N LC3), material (6063-T6, 214 MPa yield), and stress limits (107 / 107 / 143 MPa) are preserved verbatim. Mass cap 4 kg is the total assembled frame mass (A+B+C+D+...).
short_prompt:
Design the open-frame chassis for a SAUVC (Singapore AUV Challenge) AUV as a multi-part bolted extrusion assembly, compliant with SAUVC Rulebook v6. Vehicle bounding box 1500 mm x 800 mm x 500 mm; pool-depth rated 5 m; vehicle dry mass 35 kg.

Frame is bolted extruded 6063-T6 aluminum (25 x 25 mm square, 3 mm wall). Material: 6063-T6 (E = 68.3 GPa, yield = 214 MPa, ultimate = 241 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3).

Carries 6 thrusters (each nominal 50 N static, peak 80 N), 1 pressure hull (8 kg dry), 1 battery pod (4 kg dry), acoustic array (2 kg). Total applied-in-frame mass 35 kg.

LC1 handling: 35 kg lifted by a single top handle at mid-length => 343 N concentrated with 2x dynamic amp = 687 N; max von Mises <= yield / 2.0 = 107 MPa. LC2 thruster combined: 6 thrusters at peak 80 N in worst combination (2 surge + 2 sway + 2 heave); max von Mises <= 107 MPa. LC3 impact: 500 N at any corner; max von Mises <= yield / 1.5 = 143 MPa. LC4 hydrostatic buoyancy at 5 m depth (50 kPa external): deflection on any closed-volume member <= 0.5 mm (geometric). Frame structural mass <= 4 kg. Kill-switch reachable from any accessible face within 500 mm (geometric).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Corner-bracket and thruster-cradle bolt utilization under LC1-LC3 is a VDI 2230 hand-calc on top of FEA reactions.

prompt.title: SAUVC AUV open-frame chassis (multi-part bolted assembly)
prompt.brief: Al 6063-T6 25 x 25 x 3 wall extrusion open cage for a 35 kg AUV at 5 m pool depth. Multi-part: forward ring (A), aft ring (B), longitudinal tubes (C x6), thruster cradles (D x6), pressure-hull saddle (E), dome mount (F), ballast tray (G), top lift handle (H). FEM assembled-only; DCR <= 1.0.

load_cases.name: handling_lift
load_cases.applied_to: H top lift handle at mid-length of top longitudinals

load_cases.name: thruster_combined
load_cases.applied_to: D thruster cradles, worst-combined 2 surge + 2 sway + 2 heave

load_cases.name: corner_impact
load_cases.applied_to: any corner of A or B ring

boundary_conditions.mounting: for LC1, the handle H is fixed and the remainder of the frame is free; for LC2, the pressure-hull saddle E is fixed (reacting thruster net force through the saddle to the vehicle mass) and thruster cradles D carry the applied forces; for LC3, the opposite corner is fixed and the impact load is applied to the free corner
boundary_conditions.bolted_joints: A<->C, B<->C corner brackets modeled as stiffness-equivalent MPC/CBUSH pairs with bolt-shear post-check

output_format.body_count: multi-body (A + B + C x6 + D x6 + E + F + G + H)

parts.name: forward_ring_frame
parts.role: rectangular hoop at the bow tying the four forward ends of the longitudinals; carries dome mount F and forward thruster cradle

geometric_constraints.corner_brackets: 4x Al 6061 machined brackets joining A to C

parts.name: aft_ring_frame
parts.role: rectangular hoop at the stern tying the four aft ends of the longitudinals; carries aft thruster cradle

geometric_constraints.corner_brackets: 4x Al 6061 machined brackets joining B to C

parts.name: longitudinal_tube
parts.role: longitudinal extrusion bar tying the forward and aft rings; six instances (top-port, top-stbd, bottom-port, bottom-stbd, midline-port, midline-stbd)

geometric_constraints.attachment: bolted at each end to corresponding A/B corner bracket

parts.name: thruster_cradle
parts.role: aluminum plate carrying the thruster bore, bolted to the adjacent longitudinals/ring; six instances one per face

parts.name: pressure_hull_saddle
parts.role: strap saddle + backplate carrying the 8 kg pressure hull via the two bottom longitudinals

parts.name: dome_mount
parts.role: forward-facing plate bolted to A ring, carrying the camera / acoustic-array dome (~2 kg)

parts.name: ballast_tray
parts.role: tray bolted to the bottom longitudinals carrying the battery pod (~4 kg) and removable ballast

parts.name: top_lift_handle
parts.role: transverse handle bar bolted across the top longitudinals at mid-length; primary LC1 load-point

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X forward (surge), Y port-starboard (sway), Z up (heave)

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection DCR across bolted interfaces (IF1..IF7) <= 1.0 under the governing load case.
Corner-bracket bolts (A<->C, B<->C) bolt utilization <= 1.0 under LC1 (worst-case handle lift).
pass_fail_criteria.metric: max bolt utilization at IF1, IF2

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam (B31) for extrusions; solid/shell for cradles D and saddle E
connection_modeling: corner brackets as rigid zone + stiffness-equivalent MPC joint
thruster cradle bolts as CBUSH pairs
saddle E as connected solid with strap clamp force applied

interfaces.mediated_by: Al 6061 corner bracket + 4 M6 A2-70 bolts per corner
interfaces.load_path: LC1 lift and LC2 thruster reactions transmit between forward ring and longitudinals

interfaces.mediated_by: Al 6061 corner bracket + 4 M6 bolts per corner
interfaces.load_path: aft-end closure of the cage; primary LC3 impact reaction if the aft corner is struck
thruster reaction into the adjacent frame member; governs LC2
carries the 8 kg pressure hull; becomes the fixed BC under LC2 thruster net-force check
camera/acoustic-array dead weight into forward ring
battery + ballast dead weight into bottom longitudinals
primary LC1 handle-lift load into top longitudinals; governs R1

fem_check_policy.rationale: The frame is a bolted bar cage; max stresses concentrate at the corner brackets and the cradle/saddle bolt patterns. A single-body idealisation with welded joints would under-estimate stresses at these bolted nodes.

notes.why_multi_part: SAUVC frames are always bolted cage assemblies for mission-pack serviceability; the single-body STEP in the brief was an FEA-only idealization.
notes.speculation_caveats: Longitudinal count (6) covers top, bottom, and midline rails; some teams use 4 (top+bottom only) or 8 (with diagonal ties).

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Not applicable (no closed pressure volumes in frame).
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Extrusion sections sized well above slender ratios.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.

fea_experiments.metric: max bolt utilization at IF1, IF2
